# Complete Guide: Using cURL for REST API Testing in Ubuntu

<!-- Generated by Claude 4.6 Sonnet -->

---

## 1. Installation

```bash
# Check if cURL is installed
curl --version

# Install if not present
sudo apt update && sudo apt install curl -y
```

---

## 2. Basic Syntax

```bash
curl [OPTIONS] [URL]
```

---

## 3. HTTP Methods

### GET Request

```bash
# Basic GET
curl https://api.example.com/users

# GET with pretty output (requires python3)
curl https://api.example.com/users | python3 -m json.tool

# GET with verbose output
curl -v https://api.example.com/users
```

### POST Request

```bash
# POST with JSON body
curl -X POST https://api.example.com/users \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"name": "John", "email": "john@example.com"}'

# POST with form data
curl -X POST https://api.example.com/login \
  -F "username=john" \
  -F "password=secret"
```

### PUT Request

```bash
curl -X PUT https://api.example.com/users/1 \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"name": "John Updated", "email": "john@example.com"}'
```

### PATCH Request

```bash
curl -X PATCH https://api.example.com/users/1 \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"name": "John Patched"}'
```

### DELETE Request

```bash
curl -X DELETE https://api.example.com/users/1
```

---

## 4. Headers

```bash
# Single header
curl -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_TOKEN" https://api.example.com/users

# Multiple headers
curl https://api.example.com/users \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "Accept: application/json" \
  -H "X-Custom-Header: value"

# View response headers only
curl -I https://api.example.com/users

# View both request & response headers
curl -v https://api.example.com/users
```

---

## 5. Authentication

### Basic Auth

```bash
curl -u username:password https://api.example.com/users

# OR using header
curl -H "Authorization: Basic $(echo -n 'user:pass' | base64)" \
  https://api.example.com/users
```

### Bearer Token (JWT)

```bash
curl -H "Authorization: Bearer eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsIn..." \
  https://api.example.com/protected
```

### API Key

```bash
# As header
curl -H "X-API-Key: your_api_key_here" https://api.example.com/data

# As query param
curl "https://api.example.com/data?api_key=your_api_key_here"
```

---

## 6. Query Parameters

```bash
# Single param
curl "https://api.example.com/users?role=admin"

# Multiple params
curl "https://api.example.com/users?role=admin&page=1&limit=10"

# URL encode special characters
curl -G https://api.example.com/search \
  --data-urlencode "q=hello world" \
  --data-urlencode "lang=en"
```

---

## 7. Sending Data

### JSON Body from String

```bash
curl -X POST https://api.example.com/users \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"name": "Alice", "age": 30}'
```

### JSON Body from File

```bash
# Create a file
cat > payload.json << EOF
{
  "name": "Alice",
  "age": 30,
  "email": "alice@example.com"
}
EOF

# Send file as body
curl -X POST https://api.example.com/users \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d @payload.json
```

### Form URL-Encoded

```bash
curl -X POST https://api.example.com/login \
  -H "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded" \
  -d "username=alice&password=secret"
```

### File Upload (Multipart)

```bash
curl -X POST https://api.example.com/upload \
  -F "file=@/path/to/file.jpg" \
  -F "description=Profile photo"
```

---

## 8. Output & Saving Responses

```bash
# Save response to file
curl https://api.example.com/users -o response.json

# Show only response body (silent mode)
curl -s https://api.example.com/users

# Show response with HTTP status code
curl -s -o response.json -w "%{http_code}" https://api.example.com/users

# Pretty print JSON response
curl -s https://api.example.com/users | python3 -m json.tool

# Using jq (recommended)
sudo apt install jq -y
curl -s https://api.example.com/users | jq '.'

# Filter specific field with jq
curl -s https://api.example.com/users | jq '.[0].name'
```

---

## 9. SSL / HTTPS

```bash
# Skip SSL verification (for self-signed certs — dev only!)
curl -k https://localhost:8443/api/users

# Use custom CA certificate
curl --cacert /path/to/ca.crt https://api.example.com/users

# Use client certificate
curl --cert client.crt --key client.key https://api.example.com/users
```

---

## 10. Cookies

```bash
# Send a cookie
curl -b "session=abc123; token=xyz" https://api.example.com/dashboard

# Save cookies to file
curl -c cookies.txt https://api.example.com/login \
  -d "username=alice&password=secret"

# Load cookies from file
curl -b cookies.txt https://api.example.com/dashboard
```

---

## 11. Timeouts & Retry

```bash
# Connection timeout
curl --connect-timeout 5 https://api.example.com/users

# Max time for whole request
curl --max-time 10 https://api.example.com/users

# Retry on failure
curl --retry 3 --retry-delay 2 https://api.example.com/users
```

---

## 12. Useful Flags Cheat Sheet

| Flag               | Description                             |
| ------------------ | --------------------------------------- |
| `-X`               | HTTP method (GET, POST, PUT, etc.)      |
| `-H`               | Add header                              |
| `-d`               | Request body/data                       |
| `-o`               | Save output to file                     |
| `-s`               | Silent mode (no progress bar)           |
| `-v`               | Verbose (show full request/response)    |
| `-I`               | Fetch headers only                      |
| `-u`               | Basic auth (`user:pass`)                |
| `-k`               | Skip SSL verification                   |
| `-b`               | Send cookies                            |
| `-c`               | Save cookies to file                    |
| `-F`               | Multipart form data                     |
| `-L`               | Follow redirects                        |
| `-G`               | Force GET with `--data` as query params |
| `--data-urlencode` | URL-encode query parameters             |

---

## 13. Real-World Examples

### Login & Get Token

```bash
TOKEN=$(curl -s -X POST https://api.example.com/auth/login \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"username":"admin","password":"secret"}' | jq -r '.token')

echo "Token: $TOKEN"
```

### Use Token in Next Request

```bash
curl -s https://api.example.com/profile \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" | jq '.'
```

### Paginated Requests in a Loop

```bash
for page in 1 2 3 4 5; do
  echo "--- Page $page ---"
  curl -s "https://api.example.com/items?page=$page&limit=10" | jq '.data[]'
done
```

### Test Health Endpoint & Check Status

```bash
STATUS=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" https://api.example.com/health)

if [ "$STATUS" -eq 200 ]; then
  echo "✅ API is healthy"
else
  echo "❌ API returned: $STATUS"
fi
```

---

## 14. Recommended Tools Alongside cURL

| Tool                   | Purpose                    | Install                   |
| ---------------------- | -------------------------- | ------------------------- |
| `jq`                   | JSON parsing & filtering   | `sudo apt install jq`     |
| `httpie`               | Human-friendly HTTP client | `sudo apt install httpie` |
| `python3 -m json.tool` | JSON pretty print          | Built-in                  |
| `bat`                  | Syntax-highlighted output  | `sudo apt install bat`    |

---

> **Pro Tip:** Store your base URL and token in environment variables for cleaner commands:
>
> ```bash
> export BASE_URL="https://api.example.com"
> export TOKEN="your_bearer_token"
> curl -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" $BASE_URL/users
> ```
